prompts.prompt: My DOCA AES-GCM decrypt task came back with DOCA_ERROR_IO_FAILED on the completion. Should I just put it in a retry loop?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-aes-gcm/SKILL.md plus the doca-debug overlay (AGENTS.md mandatory trigger for any DOCA_ERROR_*). The IO_FAILED-class row in libs/doca-aes-gcm/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy says this completion status on a doca_aes_gcm_task_decrypt is the security-critical 'auth tag verification FAILED' outcome — the ciphertext was tampered with or corrupted, and the plaintext output of that task is poisoned. Per libs/doca-aes-gcm/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy (AEAD discipline) and libs/doca-aes-gcm/TASKS.md ## debug Layer 6, a retry of the same ciphertext+key+IV+AAD will fail the same way and hides the security signal — do NOT retry. Discard the plaintext, treat the input ciphertext as untrusted, log a security event (IV, AAD, upstream source — NOT the key), and route to your application-layer policy for tampered input. Verify the configuration with a published-vector encrypt+decrypt round-trip (NIST GCMVS or RFC 5288) per libs/doca-aes-gcm/TASKS.md ## test step 3 before doing any further runtime diagnosis.

prompts.prompt: Can I use AES-192-GCM through doca-aes-gcm on a BlueField-3?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-aes-gcm/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-aes-gcm/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (Key sizes — only 128 and 256), the library's `enum doca_aes_gcm_key_type` exposes only `DOCA_AES_GCM_KEY_128` and `DOCA_AES_GCM_KEY_256`. AES-192-GCM is NOT in the enum and is not expressible through this library at all — no cap query exists for it. For the two real key types, gate on `doca_aes_gcm_cap_task_encrypt_is_key_type_supported(devinfo, key_type)` and the matching `_decrypt_is_key_type_supported` per libs/doca-aes-gcm/TASKS.md ## configure step 2 before sizing key material. If AES-192-GCM is a hard requirement, route to a CPU library (OpenSSL `EVP_aes_192_gcm`); the DOCA accelerator path is not available. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-AES-GCM/ for the public DOCA AES-GCM page.

prompts.prompt: Where are the DOCA AES-GCM samples in my install and which one should I start from for a single encrypt + decrypt round-trip?
prompts.response: Per libs/doca-aes-gcm/SKILL.md ('What this skill deliberately does not ship'), the verified AES-GCM source code is the shipped C samples at `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_aes_gcm/` — the bundle does not duplicate or ship its own source. Per AGENTS.md ground rule 5, do not scaffold a `main.c` from documentation prose; pick a sample whose task direction (encrypt vs decrypt vs both) is closest to your intent (libs/doca-aes-gcm/TASKS.md ## modify slot 1) and apply a minimum-diff change via the universal modify-from-sample workflow in doca-programming-guide. Before touching the diff, confirm what is actually shipped with `ls /opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_aes_gcm/` (libs/doca-aes-gcm/TASKS.md ## Command appendix) — paste the listing back so we pick the closest sample together. For the public guide and the install-tree layout, see https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-AES-GCM/ and libs/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md.

prompts.prompt: I want to enumerate every running process on my x86 host using DOCA App Shield. Do I install something on the host?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-apsh/SKILL.md plus the doca-version overlay (AGENTS.md mandatory trigger). Per libs/doca-apsh/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (side split — DPU vs host), App Shield is asymmetric: the entire App Shield program — `doca_apsh_*` calls, the loaded kernel symbol map, the `doca_apsh_system` + `doca_apsh_proc` enumerator objects — runs on the BlueField Arm side; the host runs nothing at all for App Shield (no agent, no kernel module, no DOCA library). That is the appeal: a compromised host cannot disable an observer it doesn't host. Before any enumeration, you need (per libs/doca-apsh/TASKS.md ## configure steps 1–3) a host-OS-version-matching kernel symbol map loaded on the DPU side and sudo on the DPU; then call `doca_apsh_cap_*` against the active `doca_devinfo` to confirm the introspection target is supported on this host OS / kernel pair. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-App-Shield/index.html for the public guide.

prompts.prompt: My doca_apsh_module enumeration returns DOCA_ERROR_NOT_FOUND for a kernel module that `lsmod` shows on the host. Is this a bug in App Shield?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-apsh/SKILL.md plus the doca-debug overlay. Per libs/doca-apsh/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy and the cross-cutting rule in libs/doca-apsh/CAPABILITIES.md ## Pattern overview, `NOT_FOUND` is a *normal answer*, not an App Shield bug — it means the object did not exist in the host's data structures at the moment the snapshot was taken (timing race, name mismatch, namespace mismatch, or the module was unloaded between `lsmod` and the enumeration). Two non-bug causes dominate in practice and are worth ruling out first per libs/doca-apsh/TASKS.md ## debug Layer 6 (program): (a) the loaded kernel symbol map on the DPU is stale relative to the host's current kernel — App Shield was walking the previous-kernel layout and your module address is at a new offset; (b) you are querying by a slightly different name than the host registered. Verify by re-loading the symbol map (libs/doca-apsh/TASKS.md ## configure step 2) and re-running `doca_apsh_cap_*` for the module-enumeration target. If the symptom persists across a fresh map + matching kernel version, escalate via doca-debug ## debug.

prompts.prompt: I need a real-time event stream of every fork() on the host. Is DOCA App Shield the right tool?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-apsh/SKILL.md — but the answer is no. Per libs/doca-apsh/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (path-selection table), App Shield is *poll-based, not event-driven*: it produces periodic snapshots of host kernel state via the enumerator object family (`doca_apsh_proc` / `_module` / `_lib` / `_thread`), and there is no `every fork()` event hook. The table explicitly routes that requirement to a host-resident solution, or to libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md (DOCA Comch) for a host↔DPU message channel that a small custom host agent can drive — Comch is the simpler primitive when host install is acceptable. If host install is not acceptable, App Shield can give you periodic-snapshot process-set diffs, but it cannot deliver a fork-by-fork stream. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-App-Shield/index.html for the App Shield scope.

prompts.prompt: How do I add a `--my-flag <int>` to a shipped DOCA DMA sample without breaking its standard `--device <PCI>` and `--sdk-log-level <LEVEL>` flags?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-argp/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md (DMA owns the sample tree). Per libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes, the standard DOCA CLI surface (`--device <PCI>`, `--representor <name>`, `--rep-list`, `--json <path>`, `--sdk-log-level <level>`) is contributed by the Arg Parser itself; you preserve it for free by NOT replacing the sample's `doca_argp_init` / `doca_argp_start` / `doca_argp_destroy` skeleton. The minimum-diff change per libs/doca-argp/TASKS.md ## modify is: (1) call `doca_argp_param_create` for `--my-flag`, set short/long names and the value-callback that writes to your config struct, declare the type from the public enum (frontmatter spec: `DOCA_ARGP_TYPE_STRING` / `_INT` / `_BOOLEAN` / `_DEVICE` / `_DEVICE_REP` / `_DOUBLE`), (2) `doca_argp_register_param` BEFORE `doca_argp_start` — registering after start returns `DOCA_ERROR_BAD_STATE` per libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy. Verify with `./sample --help` (the registered flag appears alongside the standard ones) and the smoke from libs/doca-argp/TASKS.md ## test. AGENTS.md ground rule 5 — do not invent a `main.c`; the verified usage is the sample's `*_main.c` under `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/`.

prompts.prompt: How many parameter types can I register with doca_argp? A colleague said it's just string/int/bool, but I want to register a device flag.
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-argp/SKILL.md. There is a tension inside the bundle here that I will surface honestly: the SKILL.md frontmatter description lists SIX public parameter-type enum values (`DOCA_ARGP_TYPE_STRING`, `_INT`, `_BOOLEAN`, `_DEVICE`, `_DEVICE_REP`, `_DOUBLE`) while the SKILL.md body (`## When to load this skill` and `## What this skill provides`) describes the set as 'string, int, bool flag, JSON config file' — fewer types and treating `--json` as if it were a parameter type (it is a *CLI flag* per libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes, distinct from a registered parameter type). Per AGENTS.md ground rule 3 (never invent), the right move is: don't guess from prose — confirm against your installed header on this DOCA version (`grep -r 'enum doca_argp_type' "$(pkg-config --variable=includedir doca-argp)"`). The frontmatter list is the more specific claim, and `_DEVICE` / `_DEVICE_REP` are the canonical way a sample binds the standard `--device` / `--representor` flags. See libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility and https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Arg-Parser/index.html for the version-pinned authoritative list.

prompts.prompt: Can I drive a DOCA sample from a JSON config file instead of typing twenty flags every run?
prompts.response: Yes. Load libs/doca-argp/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes, the standard DOCA CLI surface every sample inherits includes `--json <path>` (note: the real flag is `--json`, NOT `--json-config` — the SKILL.md frontmatter calls that out explicitly). The Arg Parser reads each top-level key from the JSON file and binds it to the registered param of the same long-name; per libs/doca-argp/TASKS.md ## modify and ## run, unknown JSON keys surface as `DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE` (or `_NOT_FOUND` for a missing required key) per libs/doca-argp/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy. Smoke per libs/doca-argp/TASKS.md ## test: run the sample once with the original flag form, then once with `--json ./my-config.json` and confirm identical output. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Arg-Parser/index.html.

prompts.prompt: I want to stream a 64 KiB chunk every 100 microseconds from a host driver to a DPU agent. Should I use Comch slow-path or fast-path?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-comch/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (slow-path vs fast-path), fast-path (producer / consumer) is the right answer for this shape: it is the asynchronous, much-higher-throughput surface designed for bulk-streaming data between host and DPU. Slow-path (send-task / recv-callback) is message-oriented with single-call simplicity but lower throughput and is the wrong fit for a 64 KiB-every-100µs continuous stream. Cap-query first: `doca_comch_cap_get_max_msg_size` against the active `doca_devinfo` to confirm 64 KiB fits per submission (libs/doca-comch/TASKS.md ## configure / ## modify property-set workflow); if 64 KiB is over the cap on your device, fragment at the application layer. Bring-up: server on the DPU side, client on the host side, configure the producer / consumer contexts BEFORE `doca_ctx_start()`, then drive the progress engine. Validate with the channel-up smoke in libs/doca-comch/TASKS.md ## test before pushing the real stream.

prompts.prompt: On my DOCA 2.4 install, `pkg-config --exists doca-comch` returns failure. Did the bundle author make a typo on the module name?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md plus the doca-version overlay (AGENTS.md mandatory trigger for any 'is X on my install' question). Not a typo — per libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md (`compatibility` frontmatter and ## Version compatibility) the module was renamed in DOCA 2.5: the modern name is `doca-comch`, the pre-2.5 module is `doca-comm-channel`. So on DOCA 2.4 you'd see `pkg-config --modversion doca-comm-channel` succeed and the new module absent. Walk the canonical four-source detection chain per AGENTS.md (`pkg-config --modversion doca-common`, `cat /opt/mellanox/doca/applications/VERSION`, `doca_caps --version`, BFB on BlueField) to confirm you really are on 2.4, then either pin your build to the legacy module on 2.4 OR upgrade DOCA per libs/doca-setup/SKILL.md before using the modern `doca-comch` surface. The producer/consumer fast-path surface availability is also version-gated — confirm via the libs/doca-comch/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility overlay before assuming the modern API shape on a 2.4 host.

prompts.prompt: I'm getting DOCA_ERROR_AGAIN on `doca_comch_task_send_submit` at high rate. Should I tight-loop retry?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md plus the doca-debug overlay. Per libs/doca-comch/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy (Comch overlay), `DOCA_ERROR_AGAIN` on the slow-path send-task submit means the task queue is full — it is a *would-block, retry after progress* signal, not a hardware error and not a permanent failure. Per libs/doca-comch/TASKS.md ## debug, the right reaction is to drain completions via `doca_pe_progress()` on the same progress engine BEFORE re-submitting; a bare tight-loop retry without a progress call will burn CPU and never drain the queue. If you are seeing sustained `_AGAIN` storms at high rate, slow-path is the wrong shape — move that traffic onto the producer/consumer fast-path per libs/doca-comch/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (the slow-path vs fast-path selection rule). Smoke: confirm completions arrive between bursts on the PE per libs/doca-comch/TASKS.md ## test; if not, the PE is not being progressed at all.

prompts.prompt: I'm starting a brand-new DOCA Flow program on BlueField-3. What's the doca-common skeleton I need before I open the Flow port?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-common/SKILL.md (always loaded first) plus libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md (per-library) plus doca-version + doca-setup ## recognize overlays (AGENTS.md mandatory triggers). The universal foundation walk per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md (## dev, ## ctx, ## progress engine) and libs/doca-common/TASKS.md ## configure is: `doca_devinfo_create_list` → pick the right device by capability via `doca_*_cap_*` queries against the active `doca_devinfo` (the runtime authority — never trust docs alone, per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## dev) → `doca_dev_open` → set up `doca_mmap` + `doca_buf_inventory` + `doca_buf` for zero-copy I/O (libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## buf) → `doca_pe_create` → `doca_pe_connect_ctx` → per-library `doca_ctx_start` → submit work → drive `doca_pe_progress` → drain completions → `doca_ctx_stop` → destroy. Wire DOCA Log via the two-tier model per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## log and libs/doca-common/TASKS.md ## log BEFORE first log line. Do NOT scaffold a `main.c` from prose — AGENTS.md ground rule 5; derive from a shipped sample under `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_flow/` per libs/doca-programming-guide/TASKS.md ## modify.

prompts.prompt: I set `--sdk-log-level DEBUG` on my sample, but my own `DOCA_LOG_DBG` lines still don't print. What's going on?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-common/SKILL.md plus doca-debug overlay (AGENTS.md mandatory trigger). Per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## log (two-tier model) and libs/doca-common/TASKS.md ## log (tier-flip iteration), `--sdk-log-level` controls the *SDK-internal* log tier — DOCA library code itself — while *your* `DOCA_LOG_DBG` lines emit through the *app-side* logger registry that you registered via `DOCA_LOG_REGISTER`. The two tiers are independent: cranking the SDK tier alone does not flip the app-side tier. The fix is to also flip the app-side tier (the explicit setter per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## log, which the worked iteration in TASKS.md ## log walks). Smoke: set both tiers to DEBUG, re-run, expect both your `DOCA_LOG_DBG` line and SDK-internal debug lines; if only SDK lines appear you flipped the wrong tier, if only yours appear `--sdk-log-level` was misspelled. Keep AGENTS.md ground rule on key material in mind: trace-level can be verbose, do not log secret buffers (also surfaced for the AES-GCM key case in libs/doca-aes-gcm/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy).

prompts.prompt: My doca_rdma task submits cleanly but no completion ever arrives. What am I missing?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-common/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-rdma/SKILL.md plus doca-debug overlay. Per libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## progress engine (the universal task-completion drain rule) and libs/doca-common/TASKS.md ## run, `submitted but no completion` is almost always a missed `doca_pe_progress()` call in your main loop — the PE is event-driven but you have to *drive* it. Walk the universal 5-phase debug-loop from AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract on this symptom: (1) Layer: program (layer 6 of the 7-layer ladder, not Runtime); (2) Read-only triple: program output + `doca_pe_progress` call count + per-ctx state; (3) Single-variable change: add or re-enable the `doca_pe_progress` call on the same thread that owns the PE; (4) Re-capture: completions should now arrive; (5) Exit on the named green signal — a non-zero progress count per second and a non-zero completion-handler invocation count. Adjacent traps to rule out at the same layer: the PE is on a different thread than the one running the loop; the ctx was connected to a different PE than the one you progress; `doca_ctx_start()` was never called.

prompts.prompt: Can I offload LZ4 compression of incoming network buffers to my BlueField via doca-compress?
prompts.response: Partly. Load libs/doca-compress/SKILL.md. Per the bundle's catalog row in README.md (## Skill Catalog → DOCA Libraries) and libs/doca-compress/SKILL.md frontmatter, the DOCA Compress hardware supports DEFLATE in both directions plus LZ4 *decompress* (stream and block) — LZ4 *encode/compress* is NOT on the accelerator. So 'offload LZ4 compression' is a no-go on this library; the right answers per libs/doca-compress/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes path selection are: (a) keep LZ4 compression on a CPU LZ4 library (e.g. lz4 / lz4hc); (b) if you can switch the wire format to DEFLATE, doca-compress offloads both directions; (c) if the workload is purely *decompressing* inbound LZ4 streams, that *is* in scope — enable `doca_compress_task_decompress_lz4_stream` or `_decompress_lz4_block` (per the frontmatter task list), cap-gate via the `_is_supported` query against your `doca_devinfo`, and smoke per libs/doca-compress/TASKS.md ## test before bulk. Note the SKILL.md body section 'What this skill provides' under-describes the task set as only DEFLATE compress + DEFLATE decompress — that is a bundle-internal inaccuracy I'm flagging (the frontmatter + README catalog are correct; the body undercounts).

prompts.prompt: I receive 4 MiB DEFLATE-compressed payloads off the network. Is decompression-only a valid standalone use of doca-compress, or do I have to also enable the compress task?
prompts.response: Decompression-only is a fully valid standalone shape. Load libs/doca-compress/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-compress/SKILL.md ## When to load this skill and libs/doca-compress/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes, the compress and decompress task types are *independent* — a consumer that only decompresses inbound DEFLATE payloads enables only `doca_compress_task_decompress_deflate` (via `_set_conf` before `doca_ctx_start()`) and never enables the compress task. Cap-query first: `doca_compress_cap_task_decompress_deflate_is_supported(devinfo)` and `_get_max_buf_size(devinfo)` per libs/doca-compress/TASKS.md ## configure step 2 — 4 MiB inputs must fit under the per-submission max or be fragmented at the application layer. Permissions per libs/doca-compress/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy: source mmap `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY`, destination `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_WRITE`. Validate with a round-trip smoke (decompress a known zlib-encoded blob, bit-compare against the known plaintext) per libs/doca-compress/TASKS.md ## test before bulk.

prompts.prompt: `doca_compress_task_compress_deflate_alloc_init` is returning DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE on my first submission. How do I narrow this down?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-compress/SKILL.md plus doca-debug overlay. Per libs/doca-compress/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy (Compress overlay), `DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE` on alloc/init is almost always one of: (a) source `doca_buf` size exceeds `doca_compress_cap_task_compress_deflate_get_max_buf_size(devinfo)` — fix by fragmenting; (b) destination `doca_buf` is too small for the worst-case DEFLATE output of this input — bump destination size; (c) the source mmap is missing `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY` or destination is missing `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_WRITE` — those surface as `_NOT_PERMITTED` more typically but `_INVALID_VALUE` is possible at init. Walk AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract on this symptom: layer 6 (program), capture the cap-query snapshot + the configured src/dst sizes + the mmap permission set, change ONE variable (the most suspect — usually the buffer-size cap), re-capture, exit on green (a successful alloc + a successful submit + a `_SUCCESS` completion event on the PE). Per libs/doca-compress/TASKS.md ## debug, re-run the libs/doca-compress/TASKS.md ## test round-trip smoke first; if even that fails, the device cap is the issue, not your input shape.

prompts.prompt: I want my host to see a custom virtio-net device backed by code I run on the DPU. Which doca-devemu sub-library do I need, and what preconditions matter before any code call?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-devemu/SKILL.md plus doca-hardware-safety overlay (firmware-level emulation-type enablement is the trigger per AGENTS.md). DOCA Device Emulation is an *umbrella* covering three sub-libraries: PCI Generic (raw PCIe), virtio-net (emulated virtio NIC) and virtio-fs (emulated virtio filesystem). For your case the sub-library is virtio-net — pkg-config module per libs/doca-devemu/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (per-sub-library module name). The dual-axis precondition matrix (libs/doca-devemu/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes + ## Safety policy) requires BOTH (a) the BlueField firmware-level virtio-net emulation slot is enabled (otherwise `doca_devemu_virtio_*_create` returns `DOCA_ERROR_NOT_PERMITTED` even with sudo and a valid `doca_dev`) AND (b) `doca_devemu_virtio_cap_*` against the active `doca_devinfo` confirms this BlueField generation supports the virtio-net surface — generations differ. The host kernel must already ship a virtio_net driver (this skill does NOT install host kernel drivers). If you want a packaged daemon and not to write the backend, route via libs/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md to the DOCA Virtio-net Service guide — that is non-goal #7 territory for this library skill. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Device-Emulation/index.html.

prompts.prompt: I want NVMe storage exposed to my host. Should I write the backend with doca-devemu or use a packaged service?
prompts.response: Recognize this as a non-goal #7 routing question per AGENTS.md ## Non-goals (DOCA SNAP Services is externally-productized and not in the `doca/{libs,services,tools}/` monorepo, so out of scope for this bundle). Three-part response per AGENTS.md: (a) DOCA SNAP Services is externally-productized and out of scope for this bundle; (b) the boundary is the strict-1:1 with the monorepo rule from README.md and AGENTS.md — `doca-devemu` *is* in scope and is the framework underneath, but the packaged 'NVMe SNAP without writing the backend' daemon is not; (c) routing with substance per libs/doca-public-knowledge-map ## Externally-productized DOCA software: authoritative docs URL https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-SNAP-Services/index.html, common SNAP-enumeration gotcha classes (NIC firmware `mlxconfig`, SNAP container running on BF, fabric target reachability from BF management VRF, BF BSP / SNAP version match), and the NVIDIA Developer Forum entry point https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/c/infrastructure/doca/370 with the 'SNAP' search hint. prompts.prompt: My `doca_devemu_virtio_*` create call returns DOCA_ERROR_NOT_PERMITTED even though my DPU-side process has sudo and a valid `doca_dev`. What else could be wrong?

Per libs/doca-devemu/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy (dual-axis permission matrix) and the worked example in libs/doca-devemu/SKILL.md ## Example questions, `NOT_PERMITTED` on a virtio-net emulation create with sudo+`doca_dev` healthy almost always means the *firmware-level* virtio-net emulation slot is disabled in BlueField firmware — process privilege is necessary but not sufficient. Verify via the hardware binding-layer stanza from AGENTS.md ## Hardware binding-layer command stanza (read-only): `mlxconfig -d <bdf> q` to see the per-slot emulation enablement bits. The fix is a firmware-config change which is a hardware-state mutation — that routes through doca-hardware-safety ## modify per AGENTS.md (pre-flight inventory, OOB console, maintenance window, rollback documented, then `mlxconfig set` + cold reboot) and through libs/doca-setup/SKILL.md for the per-step `mlxconfig set` invocation. After the slot is enabled and the BlueField has rebooted, re-run the cap query (`doca_devemu_virtio_cap_*` against the active `doca_devinfo`) and the create call — the smoke from libs/doca-devemu/TASKS.md ## test. If `_NOT_PERMITTED` persists after both axes are healthy, escalate via doca-debug ## debug with the cap-query snapshot.

prompts.prompt: Copy a 64 KiB buffer from host memory to DPU memory in one task. Starting from which shipped sample, and what's the lifecycle?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-common/SKILL.md (foundation). Per libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md `## Related skills`, the canonical reference application is *DMA Copy*; the shipped samples live under `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_dma/<name>/`. Confirm what is present on YOUR install with `ls /opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_dma/` and paste the listing — do not invent a sample name (AGENTS.md ground rule 3). Lifecycle per libs/doca-dma/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes + libs/doca-dma/TASKS.md ## configure: open `doca_dev` → `doca_dma_create` → cap-query `doca_dma_cap_task_memcpy_is_supported` and `_get_max_buf_size` to confirm 64 KiB fits → `doca_dma_task_memcpy_set_conf` → register source `doca_mmap` with `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY` and destination with `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_WRITE` (libs/doca-dma/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy) → for cross-peer (host↔DPU) copies also do the `doca_mmap_export_*` step on the producer side and the matching import on the other → `doca_pe_create` + `doca_pe_connect_ctx` → `doca_ctx_start` → submit one `doca_dma_task_memcpy` → drive `doca_pe_progress` → on completion event, verify destination contents bit-for-bit (libs/doca-dma/TASKS.md ## test). See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-DMA/index.html.

prompts.prompt: Can I memcpy 16 MiB in a single `doca_dma_task_memcpy` on a ConnectX-6?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md. The bundle's rule is the same on every device: do not assume — per libs/doca-dma/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (capability-query rule), query `doca_dma_cap_task_memcpy_get_max_buf_size(devinfo)` and `_get_max_buf_list_len(devinfo)` against the active `doca_devinfo` per libs/doca-dma/TASKS.md ## configure step 2 BEFORE sizing the buffer. If 16 MiB ≤ `_get_max_buf_size`, one task is fine. If not, you have two clean options: (a) fragment the copy into chunks ≤ the cap and submit one task per chunk; (b) if `_get_max_buf_list_len(devinfo)` returns ≥ 2 and your source / destination are amenable, build a scatter-gather buffer list whose total stays under the per-task cap. AGENTS.md ground rule 3 forbids quoting a hardcoded ceiling from memory — even a CX-6-specific number — because the install profile and DOCA version matter. Smoke after sizing per libs/doca-dma/TASKS.md ## test before bulk.

prompts.prompt: Should I use doca-dma or doca-rdma to move 1 MiB from a host process to a DPU process on the same machine over PCIe?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-dma/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (path-selection rule against RDMA / Comch / CPU memcpy) and libs/doca-dma/SKILL.md ## Related skills: same-machine host↔DPU mmap-to-mmap bulk copy with no networking and no producer/consumer semantics is the canonical doca-dma fit — it goes over PCIe via the BlueField DMA engine and frees the host CPU. doca-rdma is the right answer when the copy *has to traverse the network* (RoCE / InfiniBand to a remote peer); using doca-rdma in-box for a host↔DPU same-machine copy adds QP setup and connection-state management you don't need. If your shape is producer/consumer messaging rather than a raw mmap-to-mmap copy, libs/doca-comch/SKILL.md (fast-path producer / consumer) is the better primitive. For 1 MiB over PCIe in-box: doca-dma. Smoke per libs/doca-dma/TASKS.md ## test.

prompts.prompt: I want to load a DPACC-built DPA application from my host program and launch a kernel that counts events. What's the host-side bring-up?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md plus doca-version overlay (the DOCA↔DPACC version-match rule is mandatory per AGENTS.md). Host-side bring-up per libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes + libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## configure: (1) confirm DOCA + DPACC versions match the DOCA Compatibility Policy (`pkg-config --modversion doca-dpa` + the installed `dpacc` version — both must be on the published compatible pair per libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility); (2) open `doca_dev` and confirm THIS BlueField has a DPA via `doca_dpa_cap_*` against the active `doca_devinfo` — BlueField generations differ in DPA hardware support and `_NOT_SUPPORTED` at this step is the answer, not a bug; (3) `doca_dpa_create` per-DPA-instance context; (4) load the DPACC-built `doca_dpa_app` image; (5) create a `doca_dpa_thread`; (6) launch the kernel via the `doca_dpa_kernel_launch_update_*` family with your event-counter args; (7) attach a `doca_dpa_completion` and drain it on the host PE per libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## run. Smoke first per libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## test — a hello-style kernel that returns a known counter. Derive from `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_dpa/` (AGENTS.md ground rule 5 — do not author a kernel from prose).

prompts.prompt: Inside my DPA kernel I want to do a one-sided RDMA read to a remote peer to fetch the next input buffer before continuing compute. Which library do I call from inside the kernel?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md ## When to load + libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## verbs, the in-kernel surface for RDMA from inside a DPA kernel is the DPA-side companion library `doca-dpa-verbs`. The 4-way RDMA matrix, the host-configures-QP / DPA-uses-QP coupling rule, the host-side `doca_dpa_verbs_cap_*` rule (cap-query for the SPECIFIC verb from host code BEFORE the kernel launch — never from inside the kernel), the `IO_FAILED → inspect CQE` overlay, and the climb-back rule (when the host-round-trip premise stops holding, walk back up to host-side libs/doca-rdma/SKILL.md or libs/doca-rdmi/SKILL.md) live in libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## verbs and libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## verbs. CAVEAT — bundle-internal contradiction worth flagging: libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md ## Related skills closes by saying 'No library skill ships for [doca-dpa-comms / doca-dpa-verbs] in this bundle yet' even though the same SKILL.md ## When to load explicitly points the agent at the in-bundle ## comms and ## verbs anchors (which do exist in CAPABILITIES.md and TASKS.md per my inventory). Treat the ## verbs anchor as authoritative for the DPA-side verbs workflow; for the DPA-side kernel-writing generalities the upstream DOCA DPA Verbs guide via libs/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md is the substantive reference. prompts.prompt: I'm getting DOCA_ERROR_DRIVER on a host-side `doca_dpa_kernel_launch_update_*`. Is the bug on the DOCA side, the DPACC-produced image, or the DPA processor itself?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md plus doca-debug + doca-version overlays. Per libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy (DPA overlay) and libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## debug, `_DRIVER` on a host-side launch is the layer-7 (driver / hardware) class — it usually means the layer BELOW DOCA reported failure. Three causes dominate; disambiguate in order: (1) DOCA↔DPACC version skew — re-run the canonical four-source version chain from AGENTS.md and confirm DOCA and DPACC are a compatible pair per libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility (most-common cause; the DPACC-produced image's calling convention silently drifts from what the installed `doca-dpa` runtime expects); (2) DPACC-produced image mismatched against the host-side declared entry-point shape (launch-argument count / type) — re-walk libs/doca-dpa/TASKS.md ## debug Layer 6 (program) to compare the launch-call signature against the DPA-side function signature; (3) the DPA processor itself reported failure to the driver — capture `dmesg` (libs/doca-debug command appendix) and route via libs/doca-setup/TASKS.md ## debug. Do NOT retry the launch in a loop — `_DRIVER` is deterministic on the configured pair until something changes.

prompts.prompt: I already have a DPDK app on a ConnectX host. How do I add DOCA Flow steering rules to those ports without rewriting the data-plane?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md plus doca-version overlay. Per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (bridge-vs-native selection) and libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/TASKS.md ## configure, the existing-DPDK + add-DOCA-on-top case is exactly what this bridge exists for — do not migrate the data-plane to libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md. Preconditions per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/SKILL.md `compatibility`: `pkg-config --exists doca-dpdk-bridge` AND `pkg-config --exists libdpdk` succeed AND the two versions fall in the bridge's matched-pair window (libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility — DPDK↔DOCA coupling overlay). Steps per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/TASKS.md ## configure step 3–4: (a) bring up the DPDK side normally (EAL init, port up); (b) surface the DPDK port as a `doca_dpdk_port` handle so DOCA Core / DOCA Flow see it; (c) construct DOCA Flow pipes against that handle per libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## modify; (d) for the data-plane boundary, use the mbuf ↔ `doca_buf` conversion helpers in libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (the conversion cost is real — keep it off the hot loop where possible). Smoke per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/TASKS.md ## test (small rule set, single port, drop-by-counter) before the full rule set.

prompts.prompt: `pkg-config --exists doca-dpdk-bridge` returns failure on a host where DPDK is installed separately from DOCA. Did I install the wrong DOCA profile, or is the module name different?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/SKILL.md plus doca-version + doca-setup ## recognize overlays. Per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/SKILL.md ## Language scope, the module name is `doca-dpdk-bridge` but the SKILL.md itself explicitly warns 'some DOCA releases use a slightly different module name and the agent must not guess' — so the first move is to confirm against your install, not to invent. Concretely (AGENTS.md ground rule 3 + 6): run `find /opt/mellanox/doca -name 'doca-dpdk*.pc' -path '*/pkgconfig/*'` and `find /opt/mellanox/doca -name '*dpdk*bridge*.pc'` to locate the actual `.pc` filename your install ships, and `pkg-config --list-all 2>/dev/null | grep -i dpdk` after setting `PKG_CONFIG_PATH` per the libs/doca-setup discovery walk. If nothing matches, the DOCA install profile genuinely lacks the bridge package — route to libs/doca-setup/SKILL.md to install the profile that includes it (per AGENTS.md the `doca-all` profile from the DOCA Installation Guide for Linux is the superset). Per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility, the DPDK ↔ DOCA matched-pair coupling also matters — if DPDK was installed from a different release stream than DOCA expects, the bridge package may exist but its dependencies may be unsatisfied. Paste the find output back so we don't guess.

prompts.prompt: I'm starting a fresh packet-processing project — no DPDK code yet. Should I reach for doca-dpdk-bridge or for native doca-eth?
prompts.response: Native libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/SKILL.md ## Where to start + ## Related skills (the start-fresh-vs-bridge rule) and libs/doca-dpdk-bridge/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (bridge-vs-native selection), the bridge exists specifically for the *existing-DPDK + add-DOCA-on-top* case; it is the wrong default for a fresh project because you would be inheriting DPDK's EAL bring-up, hugepages, and mbuf model just to bridge them to a `doca_buf` — a layer that has no value when you have not committed to DPDK yet. doca-eth is the DOCA-native packet-queue surface (`doca_eth_rxq` + `doca_eth_txq` directly on the device) and is the right entry point for fresh work; pair it with libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md for steering (Flow decides which packets land on which RX queue — Ethernet is the queue, Flow is the steering, they are independent libraries per libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md ## Where to start). Walk the libs/doca-setup ## recognize front-door per AGENTS.md to confirm host shape (bare-metal host x86 with ConnectX or BlueField Arm), then derive from `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_eth/` per AGENTS.md ground rule 5.

prompts.prompt: I have 8 data + 4 parity blocks; one data block was just rewritten. Do I have to re-encode all the parity from scratch?
prompts.response: No. Load libs/doca-erasure-coding/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-erasure-coding/SKILL.md ## When to load and libs/doca-erasure-coding/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (task-type table), `doca_ec_task_update` is exactly this case — it incrementally updates the K redundancy blocks when ONE source block changes, and is much cheaper than recomputing all K parity blocks via a fresh `doca_ec_task_create`. Bring-up per libs/doca-erasure-coding/TASKS.md ## configure: cap-query first (`doca_ec_cap_task_update_is_supported(devinfo)` — independent of `_task_create_is_supported`, per the per-task capability-query rule), confirm the same matrix type / N / K / block_size you used at the original encode, source mmap with `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY` for the changed source block + the affected parity blocks the update reads, destination mmap `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_WRITE` for the updated parity. Validate per libs/doca-erasure-coding/TASKS.md ## test: run an update, then run a recover smoke (drop one block, recover, bit-compare) to confirm the new parity is consistent. Per libs/doca-erasure-coding/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy, mis-matched block-size or N/K between the original create and a subsequent update is the most common failure mode and surfaces as `DOCA_ERROR_INVALID_VALUE`. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Erasure-Coding/index.html.

prompts.prompt: A disk failed and two of my four parity blocks are gone. Can DOCA reconstruct them, and how do I express that?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-erasure-coding/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-erasure-coding/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (task-type table, `doca_ec_task_recover` row), Reed-Solomon with N data + K parity tolerates ANY K simultaneous block losses — so 2 missing parity (with all 8 data + 2 surviving parity intact) is recoverable. Express it via `doca_ec_task_recover`: source `doca_buf` list is the surviving subset (data blocks + the surviving parity blocks) with `DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY`, destination `doca_buf` list is sized for the missing blocks (`DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_WRITE`), and you must reuse the same matrix type / N / K / block_size from the original encode (per libs/doca-erasure-coding/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy). Pre-flight per libs/doca-erasure-coding/TASKS.md ## configure: `doca_ec_cap_task_recover_is_supported(devinfo)` + `doca_ec_cap_get_max_block_size` + `_get_max_buf_list_len` against your `doca_devinfo`. Smoke per libs/doca-erasure-coding/TASKS.md ## test (the recover smoke: encode a tiny N+K layout, simulate the same loss pattern, recover, bit-compare against the original) BEFORE running recover on real disk data — if even the smoke fails, the configuration is wrong and recovery on production data would produce silently-wrong output.

prompts.prompt: How many task types does the DOCA EC accelerator actually expose? The skill's section headers say three (create / recover / update), but one of the body bullets mentions a fourth.
prompts.response: Excellent catch — this is a real self-contradiction inside libs/doca-erasure-coding/SKILL.md that I will surface honestly per AGENTS.md ground rule 3. The skill describes the public API set as 'three task types' in two anchor headings (## What this skill provides line 'the three task types (create / recover / update, each independently capability-gated)', and ## Loading order step 2 'the EC capability matrix, the three task types (create / recover / update)'). But ## Example questions and ## When to load name a FOURTH task — `doca_ec_task_galois_mul` — gated by `doca_ec_cap_task_galois_mul_is_supported`, and explicitly call it 'the 4th public task on the EC accelerator' (and add that the public header does NOT ship a `doca_ec_cap_get_matrix_*` family, contrary to what the prose elsewhere implies). I cannot reconcile this from inside the bundle alone — the SKILL.md prose contradicts itself, and the bundle does not expose CAPABILITIES.md's task-type table in a way that lets me confirm whether galois_mul is in fact in scope or stray prose. Right next move: verify against the installed header on this DOCA version (`grep -n 'doca_ec_cap_task_' "$(pkg-config --variable=includedir doca-erasure-coding)"/doca_erasure_coding.h`) and read the public DOCA Erasure Coding guide at https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Erasure-Coding/index.html for the version-pinned authoritative task list — don't trust either side of the SKILL.md contradiction from prose alone.

prompts.prompt: I brought up `doca_eth_rxq` cleanly but no packets ever arrive at the recv callback. What's the most common cause and where do I look?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md ## Where to start and ## Example questions, this is THE canonical first-app trap: doca-eth is the *queue* surface only; libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md owns *steering* — they are independent libraries. An empty RX queue almost always means a missing or wrong Flow rule, not an Ethernet bug. Walk libs/doca-eth/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy (steering-dependency rule) and libs/doca-eth/TASKS.md ## configure step 1 (env-prep checklist, which routes the steering side to libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md and the promiscuous-mode side to libs/doca-setup/SKILL.md as the expedient first-run alternative). Verify via the read-only triple per AGENTS.md ## universal debug-loop: (1) port up (`ip link show <iface>` or `devlink port show`); (2) traffic arriving at the port at all (`ethtool -S <iface>` counters increment); (3) the per-queue stats on the chosen RX queue — if (1) and (2) green but (3) flat, the steering on the DOCA-Flow side is missing or wrong, NOT a doca-eth program-layer bug. For the quick first-run-only unstick, kernel-side promiscuous mode (`sudo ip link set <iface> promisc on`) bypasses Flow and proves the queue side; remove it before any real workload. See libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## modify for the steering-rule install.

prompts.prompt: Which RX queue type should I pick for fixed-MTU line-rate ingress into a pre-allocated buffer ring?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md. Per the frontmatter description in libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md, the `enum doca_eth_rxq_type` has FOUR public values: `_REGULAR`, `_CYCLIC`, `_MANAGED_MEMPOOL`, `_SHARED_MEMPOOL`. CAVEAT — bundle-internal inconsistency I'm flagging: the SKILL.md body sections (## When to load, ## What this skill provides, ## Example questions) describe the RX-type taxonomy as just three values 'regular / cyclic / managed-recv', dropping `_SHARED_MEMPOOL` and using a non-canonical name `managed-recv` for what the frontmatter calls `_MANAGED_MEMPOOL`. Treat the frontmatter enum names as the canonical set, then confirm against your install: `grep -n 'enum doca_eth_rxq_type' "$(pkg-config --variable=includedir doca-eth)"/doca_eth_rxq.h`. For the workload described — fixed-MTU line-rate ingress into a pre-allocated buffer ring — `_MANAGED_MEMPOOL` or `_CYCLIC` are the typical fits (managed-mempool owns a pool of fixed-size buffers; cyclic is a ring with fixed entries); `_REGULAR` is the general-purpose case and `_SHARED_MEMPOOL` lets multiple RX queues share one pool. Cap-query first per libs/doca-eth/TASKS.md ## configure: `doca_eth_rxq_cap_is_type_supported(devinfo, type)` — not every device advertises every type. Smoke per libs/doca-eth/TASKS.md ## test before pushing line-rate traffic. See https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/DOCA-Ethernet/.

prompts.prompt: How do I send a packet through `doca_eth_txq`, and what does DOCA_ERROR_AGAIN on the submit at line rate mean?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-eth/SKILL.md. Per libs/doca-eth/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (TX submission shape — frontmatter description names `doca_eth_txq_task_send` / `_lso_send` carrying packet `doca_buf`s; there is NO `doca_eth_frame` struct in the public surface): allocate a packet `doca_buf` from your `doca_buf_inventory` over a `doca_mmap` that covers the payload (`DOCA_ACCESS_FLAG_LOCAL_READ_ONLY` is sufficient for source — libs/doca-common/CAPABILITIES.md ## buf), attach the packet bytes, submit one `doca_eth_txq_task_send` task against the queue (for segmentation-offload, use `_lso_send` and confirm `doca_eth_txq_cap_*` advertises it), drive `doca_pe_progress` to drain completion events. `DOCA_ERROR_AGAIN` on submit at line rate per libs/doca-eth/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy is the queue-full signal — it is `_AGAIN` not `_IO_FAILED`, so it is recoverable: drain completions via `doca_pe_progress()` BEFORE re-submitting (libs/doca-eth/TASKS.md ## debug; same shape as the cross-library would-block pattern). If sustained `_AGAIN` storms persist after the PE is being driven, the send-queue is undersized for the offered rate — raise the configured queue depth at libs/doca-eth/TASKS.md ## configure (the `_set_*` property knobs) or pace the producer.

prompts.prompt: How do I bring up a DOCA Flow port on representor `pf0vf0` for first-app testing on a BlueField?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-common/SKILL.md (foundation) plus doca-version + doca-setup ## recognize overlays per AGENTS.md. Per libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## configure + libs/doca-flow/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes (steering-mode selection): (1) confirm install via the four-source detection chain from AGENTS.md (`pkg-config --modversion doca-flow`, `/opt/mellanox/doca/applications/VERSION`, `doca_caps --version`, BFB on BlueField); (2) `doca_caps --list-devs` to confirm `pf0vf0` is visible and that the BlueField is in switchdev mode (eswitch mode change is a hardware-state mutation — routes through doca-hardware-safety ## modify per AGENTS.md); (3) doca-common foundation walk (`doca_devinfo_create_list` → cap-query → `doca_dev_open`) per libs/doca-common/TASKS.md ## configure; (4) `doca_flow_init` with the steering mode (HWS / SWS — pick per libs/doca-flow/CAPABILITIES.md), then `doca_flow_port_cfg_create` / `_set_*` / `doca_flow_port_start` against the `pf0vf0` representor. AGENTS.md ground rule 5 — do not author a `main.c`; derive from `/opt/mellanox/doca/samples/doca_flow/` per libs/doca-programming-guide/TASKS.md ## modify. Validate per libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## test (validate-before-commit + a one-pipe smoke). On any pipe / entry add after the port is up, cite libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## rollback as the snapshot-and-replay overlay before applying (mandatory per AGENTS.md ## Per-library rollback overlay).

prompts.prompt: I have a working doca-flow port and pipes. I want to add stateful 5-tuple connection tracking with aging timers and SNAT on outbound. Does this bundle cover that?
prompts.response: Yes — it is folded inside libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md (confirmed by SKILLS.md row 'doca-flow … Folds Flow Connection Tracking as `## flow-ct`'). Per libs/doca-flow/CAPABILITIES.md ## flow-ct and libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## flow-ct: CT layers ON TOP of an already-working doca-flow port (CT extends, does not replace); multi-axis discovery via `doca_flow_ct_cap_*` against the active `doca_devinfo` — never assume CT is on, BlueField generations + DOCA versions differ; per-port `doca_flow_ct` context model; 5-tuple match shape with VRF / VNI for overlay encaps; NAT-aware actions (SNAT, DNAT, combined). Error overlay per libs/doca-flow/CAPABILITIES.md ## flow-ct: `_BAD_STATE` on layering violations (CT pipe wrapped on a port that has not been started), `_FULL` on table saturation, `_INVALID_VALUE` on NAT conflicts. Aging-timer-driven entry eviction is configured per-port; tune via the libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## flow-ct configure overlay. CT on a previously-stateless port is a stateful-context add — per AGENTS.md ## Per-library rollback overlay (CT row) the libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## flow-ct rollback sub-section (snapshot via `doca_flow_pipe_dump` of the stateless scheme + four-step CT reversal) is mandatory in the verification contract preconditions. Smoke per libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## test before pushing real traffic.

prompts.prompt: `doca_flow_port_start` returns DOCA_ERROR_BAD_STATE. Which layer should I look at first?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md plus the mandatory link-error / runtime overlay set (doca-debug + doca-version per AGENTS.md). Per libs/doca-flow/CAPABILITIES.md ## Error taxonomy (Flow overlay) and libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## debug, `_BAD_STATE` from `doca_flow_port_start` is the lifecycle layer (program-side, layer 6 of the 7-layer ladder per libs/doca-debug). The dominant causes, in order: (a) `doca_flow_init` was never called (the global Flow init must precede any port operation); (b) `doca_flow_port_cfg_create` / `_set_*` was incomplete — at least one mandatory cfg knob unset (steering mode HWS / SWS, port_id, dev) before `_port_start`; (c) the port was already started by another agent in the same process (one start per port); (d) cross-train / mixed-install hides as `_BAD_STATE` when the headers and runtime `.so` disagree — walk the four-source version chain per libs/doca-version/CAPABILITIES.md ## Version compatibility before any program-layer fix. Walk AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract on this symptom: layer identification (program), read-only triple (program output + `doca_flow_*` lifecycle state + the version chain), single-variable change (start with the lifecycle cause — likely missing `doca_flow_init` or incomplete `_port_cfg_*`), re-capture, exit on green (port shows `STARTED` + the first pipe validates successfully per libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## test).

prompts.prompt: I want my Flow pipe counters to be readable from a DPA kernel inline, without round-tripping through the host. Which library bridges Flow and DPA?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md and libs/doca-dpa/SKILL.md (this library is a bridge that depends on both). Per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md ## Where to start, this is exactly its purpose — export an existing host-side `doca-flow` pipe (or external resource: index-selector / memory) into BlueField DPA address space so a DPACC-built kernel can read counters, mutate hash-pipe entries, and update / read memory or index-selector resources inline with Flow. Preconditions per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md `compatibility`: a BlueField with a DPA processor visible to the host (the SKILL.md explicitly states 'ConnectX-only hosts cannot use this library'), DOCA + DPACC installed at a compatible pair per the DOCA Compatibility Policy (inherited from libs/doca-dpa), an already-running `doca-flow` port AND host-side `doca-dpa` execution context. Bring-up per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes + libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/TASKS.md ## configure: per-port `doca_flow_dpa_ctx`, allocate the three queue types (general / resources-write / resources-read), then walk the order-sensitive export handshake `doca_flow_dpa_pipe_export_prepare` → add entries → `_pipe_export` → `_pipe_get_device_addr`. Pass the device address to the DPA kernel (libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/TASKS.md ## run) so the kernel can call `doca_flow_pipe_hash_*` / `doca_flow_external_resource_*` / `doca_flow_queue_poll_completion`.

prompts.prompt: I called `doca_flow_dpa_pipe_export_prepare` AFTER I had already added entries to my Flow pipe, and now my DPA kernel never sees any entries. What did I get wrong?
prompts.response: Load libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md plus libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md plus doca-debug overlay. Per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md ## Example questions (worked example) and libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/CAPABILITIES.md ## Safety policy (lifecycle ordering rule), `_export_prepare` MUST be called BEFORE entries are added to the pipe — that is the order-sensitive export handshake's first step (`_export_prepare` → add entries → `_export` → `_get_device_addr`, per the frontmatter description). Calling `_export_prepare` after entries already exist 'succeeds' in the sense that no `DOCA_ERROR_*` is returned, but the prepared snapshot does not include the pre-existing entries — the DPA kernel sees an empty exported pipe and the bug is silent. Fix per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/TASKS.md ## test (staged-export workflow): tear the pipe down (rollback via libs/doca-flow/TASKS.md ## rollback snapshot-first discipline — mandatory per AGENTS.md ## Per-library rollback overlay), re-create the pipe, call `_export_prepare` FIRST against the empty pipe, then add entries, then `_pipe_export` + `_pipe_get_device_addr`, then re-launch the DPA kernel. Smoke: a single-entry hash pipe should appear in the DPA kernel; if it does not, walk AGENTS.md ## The universal debug-loop contract on the host-side state (was `_export_prepare` really called pre-entries-add?) before mutating again.

prompts.prompt: Can I use doca-flow-dpa-provider on a ConnectX-only host with no BlueField?
prompts.response: No. Load libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md. Per the SKILL.md `compatibility` frontmatter, the library 'Requires DOCA SDK installed at /opt/mellanox/doca on Linux ... with a BlueField DPU exposing a DPA processor visible to the host' and explicitly states 'ConnectX-only hosts cannot use this library'. The reason per libs/doca-flow-dpa-provider/SKILL.md ## Where to start: this library exports a `doca-flow` pipe into a DPA processor's address space so a DPA kernel can mutate it inline — without a DPA processor (which lives on BlueField only, gated on the BlueField *generation* per libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## Capabilities and modes), there is nothing to export to. If your hardware is ConnectX-only, your real options per AGENTS.md ## Non-goals routing are: (a) use libs/doca-flow/SKILL.md on its own from the host CPU — programmable steering still works, you just can't push the per-packet decision into a DPA kernel; (b) if the latency premise of doing the work in DPA was wrong (rare on ConnectX-class workloads where host CPU is fine), the climb-back rule from libs/doca-dpa/CAPABILITIES.md ## verbs applies — stay on host-side Flow; (c) if a DPA is a hard requirement, you need BlueField hardware — route via libs/doca-public-knowledge-map/SKILL.md to the BlueField product pages and DPA capability matrix on https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/.
